input.headline: Giant spider web found in Greek-Albanian border cave

raw.headline: Giant spider web found in Greek-Albanian border cave

winner: It's a relief to hear the cave is finally using natural resources for pest control instead of hiring that one guy from accounting.

candidates.text: Why did the Greek-Albanian border cave get a speeding ticket? It was caught spinning webs way too fast—apparently, even spiders can’t ignore the limit when they’re weaving across national boundaries.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Creative twist on national boundaries and spider webs, with a clever pun on 'speeding ticket' and 'spinning webs.' Brevity and specificity are well-balanced."}

candidates.text: Why did the spider get a visa? Because it wanted to set up shop at the Greek-Albanian border—turns out, even arachnids love customs lines.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Creative twist on immigration humor with specific reference to the border location."}

candidates.text: I'm so glad I'm on the side of the cave that's in Greece right now—I don't want to get tangled in the next international incident over a spider web!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Witty twist on geopolitical tensions with a spider web, concise and surprising."}

candidates.text: Why did the spider get a ticket? Because he was caught in a *web* of traffic between Greece and Albania!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.0, "reason": "Pun on 'web' is clever and ties to the headline, with a touch of geopolitical humor."}

candidates.text: It's a relief to hear the cave is finally using natural resources for pest control instead of hiring that one guy from accounting.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat creative with the 'natural resources for pest control' twist, but the reference to 'that one guy from accounting' feels a bit forced and less specific to the headline. It's not offensive, but lacks tighter connection to the spider web context."}
